title: Thermal Physics and Ideal Gases
description: Temperature, internal energy, specific heat and latent heat, the ideal gas laws, and the kinetic theory of gases.
objectives: Convert between Celsius and Kelvin temperature scales and state the significance of absolute zero
Define internal energy and calculate energy transfers using specific heat capacity (Q = mcΔT)
Calculate energy transfers during changes of state using specific latent heat (Q = mL)
Apply the ideal gas equation pV = nRT and pV = NkT, and identify Boyle's, the pressure, and Charles's laws as special cases
State the assumptions of the kinetic theory of gases and apply the kinetic theory equation pV = (1/3)Nm⟨c²⟩
Relate the mean kinetic energy of a gas molecule to its absolute temperature

lessons.title: Temperature, specific heat capacity and latent heat
